title: Modern Algebra Section 4.6
author: Hudson & Silvester
description: A snippet from Section 4.6 of Chapter 4 of 'Modern Algebra' by Hudson & Silvester. This excerpt reconstructs the lost 1895 work of Bach and Bernul on the Railway Points Problem, introducing an algebraic calculus of routes. It translates unidirectional railway switches into directed graphs and proves that the minimum number of switches required for a perfect n-track junction is exactly 2(n-1).
